key_corrections: UNVERIFIED: '68% retail cashless in 2025' — no primary BNM/PayNet source. Use verified BNM AR 2025: 18.4B e-payment transactions, 538 per capita, RM831B retail value.
INCORRECT: '42% in 2020' — BNM Financial Sector Blueprint shows 42% refers to electronic payments as share of GDP by 2020, NOT retail transactions. BNM AR 2020: 170 e-payments per capita.
INCORRECT: 'DuitNow QR 1.2 billion transactions / RM85 billion' — BNM AR 2025: DuitNow QR doubled to 3 BILLION transactions in 2025; ~3M touchpoints. BNM AR 2024: end-2024 value RM31.1B.
MISLEADING: '28 million users' — matches Touch 'n Go's verified registered users (BusinessToday Dec 2024), not all-Malaysia.
UNVERIFIED: 'TNG and GrabPay 62% combined share' — third-party survey shows TNG ALONE at ~62% preference, different metric.
INCORRECT: '4.2 cashless transactions daily, up from 1.8 in 2020' — BNM: 538/year in 2025 = ~1.47/day; 170/year in 2020 = ~0.47/day. Draft figures unsupported.

INCORRECT: '35% rural vs 78% urban' — PayNet 2022 shows 36.2% urban and 63.5% non-urban STILL USING CASH for daily expenses; 78% refers to Malaysians overall preferring cash in Q4 2021. Draft conflates cash-use with cashless-adoption.

MISLEADING: 'East Malaysia and rural Peninsular lag significantly' — PayNet 2022: businesses in Northern, Central, and East Malaysia were moving to digital; East Coast and Southern still preferred cash.

INCORRECT: 'RM2.8 billion in e-wallet fraud, up 85%' — BNM AR 2025: RM2.8B is total scam impact, not e-wallet specific. Home Ministry: RM1.57B online fraud in 2024. BNM also reported RM1.2B fraud losses AVERTED in 2025.

MISLEADING: 'Unauthorized transactions and social engineering fastest growing' — BNM Financial Stability Review 1H 2025 says OPPOSITE: unauthorised scam cases were CURBED while authorised social engineering scams rose.
INCORRECT: 'Fraud growth outpaces transaction growth, indicating worsening per-transaction risk' — compares non-equivalent datasets.
verified_facts_to_use: BNM AR 2025: 18.4 billion e-payment transactions in 2025, 538 per capita, RM831 billion retail e-payment value
BNM AR 2025: DuitNow QR doubled to 3 billion transactions; ~3 million touchpoints
BNM AR 2024: end-2024 DuitNow QR acceptance points 2.6 million, transaction value RM31.1 billion
BNM Financial Inclusion Framework 2023-2026: cash reliance persists among low-income, elderly, rural communities
BNM 2025 Basic Banking Services policy: requires cash withdrawal and deposit services to be maintained
BNM AR 2025: RM1.2 billion fraud losses averted in 2025 — 12 times actual losses reported to BNM
BNM Financial Stability Review 1H 2025: unauthorised scams curbed; authorised social engineering rising
NSRC 2025: 146,167 calls handled, 138,915 accounts frozen, RM34.53 million recovered (NSRC under direct police leadership 24/7)
PayNet 2022 Digital Payments Insights Study: 48.4% of daily expenses still paid in cash; 36.2% urban / 63.5% non-urban still using cash
overall_assessment: Not publication-ready. Most headline numbers wrong in context. Biggest failures: 68% retail share, 42% comparison, 1.2B/RM85B DuitNow QR, 28M attribution, 4.2/day, 35%/78% rural-urban, RM2.8B mischaracterised as e-wallet fraud, social engineering trend backwards. Needs full numerical rebuild from primary sources.
